en.title: plagiarism checker
en.prompt: I want you to act as a plagiarism checker. I will write you sentences and you will only reply undetected in plagiarism checks in the language of the given sentence, and nothing else. Do not write explanations on replies. My first sentence is [text to check for plagiarism]
en.remark: Determine whether the input sentence exists in the ChatGPT database.

related.title: language literary critic
related.remark: Analyzing and interpreting literary works, while providing their origins and influence.

related.title: lunatic
related.remark: Play the role of a crazy person and reply with meaningless and illogical sentences.

related.title: sentiment analysis
related.remark: Detect text sentiment: positive, neutral or negative.

metaDescription: Worried about duplicate content? Check sentences against AI databases. Verify if text is likely to be flagged as plagiarism or AI-generated instantly.

faq.q: Can the AI actually check for plagiarism?
faq.a: No. It only guesses whether a sentence resembles patterns it saw during training. There is no actual plagiarism database to query. For real plagiarism detection, use dedicated tools like CNKI, Turnitin, or Grammarly. This prompt only works as a stylistic "originality sanity check."

faq.q: So what is this prompt actually good for?
faq.a: Its main value is getting the AI to judge whether a sentence feels like a "common platitude." If the reply says detected, it means the sentence is very common (like "with the development of society") and worth rewriting for uniqueness. But it cannot prove that content is original the other way around.
